mnemonics.title: Attribute Types Memory Aid
mnemonics.content: SCoDeM: Simple/Composite + Derived/Entity + Multi-valued. Remember: SImple = Atomic, COMPosite = Parts, DERived = Calculate, Multi = Many values.

mnemonics.title: ER Diagram Notation
mnemonics.content: Rectangle = Entity, Ellipse = Attribute, Diamond = Relationship. Double means weak/multi. Dashed means derived. Underline means key.

mnemonics.title: Cardinality Types
mnemonics.content: 1:1 = One to One, 1:N = One to Many, M:N = Many to Many. Think: 1 = single line, N = crow's foot symbol.

acronyms.usage: ER Model is the conceptual framework for database design
Visual representation of entities and their relationships
Unique identifier for entity in database table

visualTips.visualization: Key attribute has underline (or double underline for primary key in some notations). Visualize as: name (normal), roll_no (key underlined)
Weak entity needs owner: draw double rectangle + relationship diamond, both with double lines. The combined key is owner.PK + weak.partial_key
Single line = optional (partial), Double line = mandatory (total). Think of single line as 'can exist alone' and double line as 'must connect'.

analogies.analogy: Entity Type is like a CLASS blueprint (STUDENT), Entity Set is like all OBJECTS created from that class (actual students in college)
Weak entity is like a CHILD - cannot exist independently, needs PARENT (strong entity). Child is identified by parent's name + child's name together.
Derived attribute is like a CALCULATED field in a spreadsheet - not stored but computed when needed, like SUM or AVERAGE formulas.
